description: From insect to horse, 'nolika' covers the broad zoological class of animals. Sentience and relation with people take their own claims. 'limoe' (creature) suits a being whose kind remains open; 'phireo' (plant) and 'tokemi' (fungus) cover two other familiar kinds of life.
articulatory_notes: Dental 'n' begins with the tongue tip behind the upper teeth and voice flowing through the nose into rounded 'o'. The tongue moves back to the alveolar ridge for stressed lateral 'li', then farther back for the brief 'k' closure and final open 'a'.
usage_notes: Plural 'lo nolika' needs no classifier. A count can insert optional 'lipha', as in 'wi lipha nolika' (two animals), or stay bare as 'wi nolika'. Habitat comes first in 'shelira nolika' (forest animal).

examples.translation: An animal dwells in the forest.

semantic_domains.nature: the broad zoological category of animal life
